title: Houstonia longiflora
taxonomy: Houstonia longiflora is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Houstonia, which consists of various species of small flowers. The species Houstonia longiflora is native to the southeastern United States and is typically found in moist, woodland environments. It is a relatively small plant, with delicate white or pink flowers that bloom in the spring. By classification, Houstonia longiflora is a member of the order Gentianales.

intro: Houstonia longiflora, commonly known as the long-flowered bluet, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. The long-flowered bluet has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits.
